tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine in both serum and blood, significant proteinuria, and high urine microalbumin/creatinine ratio, indicating impaired renal function and possible nephrotic syndrome or advanced kidney disease. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color, with low specific gravity. Blood glucose and HbA1c are not elevated, making diabetes unlikely. Prompt nephrology evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
